Post_Title: I'm honestly scared AI is going to make my role redundant in less than 2 years. How are people future-proofing your jobs?
Comment: i realize you intended this to be condescending and belittling but you actually asked an excellent question and highlighted the exact i know everything mentality people who do not actually have ai literacy tend to tout that will ultimately hurt them professionally so ill answer you anyway incase you have genuine curiosity there are levels of literacy in ai level 1 consumer usage this is basically recognition without mastery you kind of get what ai can do but you have no idea how or why the level of true literacy here is functional familiarity you enter a prompt and get an output likely does not have much knowledge on parameters token limits iterative refinement level 2 applied prompt engineering you understand enough of the mechanics of influence to shape behavior through structure you evolve from asking to instructing you use roletone framing few shot prompting chain of thought scaffolding you understand context windows token budgets chunking strategies you can design reusable frameworks that drive outputs level 3 personal workflow automation systemic applied intelligence to your use you no longer do manual prompting alone you move toward structured integration you integrate multiple tools design multi step processes build templates have templates stored for tasks etc level 4 builder youre gaining a little bit of literacy now if you were learning to read you would have some beginner books to practice sight words now you orchestrate flows not tasks youre starting to get the architectural awareness you have api integration and authentication principles you are building workflows via low code platforms you can manage webhooks retries triggers structured error handling add dashboards and analytics you dont use a tool you build an environment level 5 application development and agentic systems you are now creating systems that think and act within rules you understand the engineering and behavioral architecture you know the fundamentals to achieve this tool usage chains function calling multi step planning model selection base vs fine tuned vs rag embeddings retrieval flow vector database differentiating between declarative interactions and dynamic agents level 6 technical fluency you can evaluate claims and architecture with analytic rigor you read code and question assumptions you know when to be skeptical you understand fully how llms are trained how related documents for that purpose would best be spaced how often words should repeat for optimal result you know differentiation between foundation models fine tunes and wrapped endpoints reading jsonapi outputs and basic code for troubleshooting quantitative evaluation of model performance like accuracy latency hallucination rates you can act as a technical evaluator able to identify vapoware and risk level 7 governance ethics regulatory architecture you can design guardrails so intelligence scales safely youre now in a policy engineering layer where you connect system design to real world accountability you can author ethical and regulatory frameworks acceptable use data privacy bias testing you understand how model risk fits within governance structures transparent explainability and audit trace design if you are in a regulated industry that is adapting ai you understand domain specific compliance needs level 8 enterprise ai strategy and transformation ai literacy evolves into a systemic intelligence design you embed ai into business dna you can model workforce shifts and productivity reallocation youre routinely building investment cases tied to revenue margin and risk metrics you are structuring transformation projects and leading organizational change management its an operating model more than an initiative that benchmark is moving as new capabilities arise there is a massive difference between a level one user who has downloaded claude chat gpt and gemini and a level 8 user who can maintain compliance governance etc in theory one of those people can make decisions and design programs that can save organizations a ton of money even in extremely regulated domains like healthcare and mortgage and drive profitability the other is according to you no better than a monkey hence why literacy matters
